comment.body:
My two cents, and just a guess - Media distraction from the 30 year auction. The longer duration is most at risk from bond vigilantes with higher CPI/PPI. Needed to buy a month.. 

Responding to the correct post

But would make sense to me (especially with the new Treasury lead being from a hedge fund.) Hot macro data would normally spook the 30 yield to sell off. But as Alma has alluded to before (going out to potentially the March expiry) the market has a playbook for the most part for the new administrations first quarter. You see this too with President trying to build up the news pre-market open but the resulting move was a nothing burger

comment.body: Agreed the stuff in latest post was risk 101, up to now I’ve used heuristics to manage risk. (Max 1% risk per day etc.) but that leads to over risk sometimes if two many positions are on. That’s why I’m really interested in the next post on risk with the more complex models. Especially for when I end up holding too many options.

comment.body:
Yeah, this is a solid intro to the rabbit hole, but we’re nowhere near deep inside yet. I’m guessing she doesn’t wanna overload people with convex risk metrics just yet ..Especially those who are still stuck on naive GEX and already find her daily posts too complex. 
Tbh, I’m sure we’ll see some unsubscribes in the future when the lotto traders start dropping off. Really hope Alma doesn’t dumb it down for them.

comment.body:
I'm real curious about that log-optimized portfolio she hinted at with the Kelly Criterion. That’s a murky area, but from an engineering standpoint, there’s a ton of potential in it. 
You’re basically mapping probabilities, skewness, using options convexity, gamma/theta ratios, and how vol impacts the Greeks long-term to calculate P/L ratios at given probabilities.. Which you can also stochastically estimate ahead of time.
comment.raw_body:
I'm real curious about that log-optimized portfolio she hinted at with the Kelly Criterion. That’s a murky area, but from an engineering standpoint, there’s a ton of potential in it.

comment.body:
Then you throw GARCH into the mix to forecast future risk & beta shifts. That’s why beta convexity matters, but it’s not as straightforward algorithmically as Black-Scholes Greeks.
And off that, you build a trading algo.
